title: elaeocarpus elliffii
intro: Elaeocarpus elliffii, also known as the Pink Quindaloo, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae. It is native to New Guinea and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and are believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Elaeocarpus elliffii is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves and bark can also be used in traditional medicine to make remedies for various ailments.
